Research appears on screen
while you're still asking the question

PodPal listens to your live conversation and feeds you real-time facts, stats, and follow-up questions — on a second screen while you record. Sound like you've done 10 hours of prep. Even when you haven't.

PodPal — Live Session Recording
Talking Points
What's driving the exponential improvement in model reasoning?
How does this compare to Moore's Law predictions from 10 years ago?
Where does Africa fit into the global AI infrastructure race?
Live Research
Perplexity · AI Compute Economics
• Training compute doubled every 6 months 2010-2022, now accelerating
• GPT-4 training cost ~$100M; next frontier models estimated $1B+
• Africa hosts <1% of global GPU capacity despite 17% of world population
...so the interesting thing about the scaling laws is that nobody actually predicted how far they'd go. Most researchers thought we'd hit a wall by now, but what we're seeing instead is that the returns haven't diminished, they've actually accelerated in some domains...
Speak naturally
PodPal listens in the background. No buttons, no prompts.
HAPPENS HERE
Research appears live
Facts, stats, follow-up questions — within 5 seconds of the topic shifting.
You look brilliant
Your guest has no idea. Your audience gets a better show.
The Problem

Great podcasters fly blind.
Until now.

The best podcast conversations feel effortless. They're not. Behind every sharp follow-up is hours of prep. Behind every fact-check is a researcher. Most podcasters have neither.

A guest drops a stat you can't verify

Do you challenge it and risk being wrong? Or let it slide and look uninformed? Without a way to check in real time, you're stuck.

The perfect follow-up comes too late

The best question you had was the one you thought of three minutes after they answered. You didn't know enough to ask it in the moment.

⏰

3-5 hours of prep per episode

Researching guests, reading their books, checking their claims. It's a second job. And after all that, topics still shift in directions you didn't prepare for.

The Interface

Everything you need.
Right there on screen.

How It Works

Set up in 2 minutes.
Feels like a superpower.

1

Tell PodPal about you

Your name, your show, your usual topics. Takes 60 seconds. PodPal learns your style and what you care about.

2

Load your guest

Add your guest's name and PodPal pre-loads their key positions, notable work, and known talking points before you hit record.

3

Record as normal

PodPal runs on a second screen beside you. You glance at it like you'd glance at notes - but these notes update live as the conversation moves.

4

Conversation catches fire

Live research appears. Suggested questions update. Fact-check alerts pop when something needs verifying. Your guest thinks you're brilliant. You are.

Features

Everything happening at once.
None of it distracting.

Real-time transcription

Under 300ms latency. Every word transcribed as it's spoken, with a rolling strip at the bottom so you can always see what was just said.

Live research panel

When the topic shifts, PodPal fetches current research within seconds. Facts, statistics, recent developments - all surfaced automatically.

Suggested questions

Three follow-up questions update in real time based on what's being discussed. Never run out of places to take the conversation.

Fact-check alerts

When a guest makes a specific factual claim, PodPal flags it with a confidence score. Stay accurate without breaking the flow.

Guest intel

Pre-loaded guest profiles. Key works, known positions, controversies, and recent activity - ready before the first question.

Host memory

PodPal knows what you've said on topics before. No more accidentally contradicting yourself or repeating old material to returning listeners.

The best podcast hosts seem like they know everything. They don't. They just never get caught not knowing it. PodPal is how you get there.
Mic Mann · Host, Singularity South Africa Podcast · PodPal Beta User

FAQ

Common questions.

Does PodPal hear my guest, or just me? +

Right now PodPal uses your microphone directly. For remote recordings (Zoom, Riverside, Squadcast), it hears your voice only. Dual-source capture — so PodPal hears both sides of the conversation — is our #1 feature in development. Expected in the next release.

Does it work with Zoom, Riverside, Squadcast? +

Yes — PodPal runs in a browser tab alongside your recording software. Open it on a second monitor, start listening, then record as normal in Zoom or Riverside. They don't interfere with each other. PodPal currently picks up your mic input.

Is my audio uploaded or stored anywhere? +

No audio is uploaded or stored. Transcription happens locally in your browser using the Web Speech API. Only the text of what was said is sent to our AI research layer — never the raw audio. Your episodes stay yours.

What browser does it work on? +

Chrome and Edge work best for real-time transcription. Safari has partial support. Firefox is not supported yet — we're working on it. PodPal works on Mac, Windows, and Linux. Mobile support is in progress.

How is this different from just using ChatGPT before my show? +

ChatGPT is preparation. PodPal is presence. No amount of pre-show research helps when your guest takes the conversation somewhere unexpected — which they always do. PodPal responds to what's actually being said, right now, in the room. It's the difference between a briefing document and a researcher sitting beside you during the interview.

What if my internet drops mid-episode? +

Transcription keeps running locally. Research cards pause until connection restores, then catch up automatically. The transcript strip at the bottom continues so you always have a record of what was said. Your recording in Riverside or Zoom is completely unaffected.

Can I cancel anytime? +

Yes. No contracts, no lock-in. Cancel from your account settings anytime and you won't be billed again. Beta subscribers keep their $19/month price for as long as they stay subscribed — but there's no penalty for leaving.
